questions.question: Which pair of ratios are proportional?
questions.explanation: 6×2 = 12 and 3×4 = 12, so the ratios are proportional.

questions.question: Two ratios a:b and c:d are proportional if:
questions.explanation: Cross multiplication condition for proportion is a×d = b×c.

questions.question: A map scale of 1 : 60,00,000 means:
questions.explanation: 1 cm on map = 60,00,000 cm = 60 km.

questions.question: Which of the following is a ratio with four terms?
questions.explanation: 8:4:2:1 contains four terms.

questions.question: If 5 parts equal 10 litres, then 1 part equals:
questions.explanation: 10 ÷ 5 = 2 litres.

questions.question: What is the sum of the angles in a triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180°.

questions.question: If 180° is divided in the ratio 1:3:5, the smallest angle is:
A pie chart represents:
questions.explanation: Pie charts show proportions of a whole using circular sectors.

questions.question: The total angle in a pie chart is:
questions.explanation: A full circle has 360°.

questions.question: If 12 out of 40 students got grade A, the pie chart angle is:
Direct proportion means:
questions.explanation: In direct proportion, both quantities change in the same direction.

questions.question: Inverse proportion means:

options.C: One increases while the other decreases

questions.explanation: Inverse proportional quantities change in opposite directions.

questions.question: If speed increases, time taken to travel the same distance:
questions.explanation: Speed and time are inversely proportional.

questions.question: Which is an example of inverse proportion?
questions.explanation: More workers mean less time required.

questions.question: If 20 workers complete work in 4 days, 10 workers will take:
questions.explanation: Half workers require double time.

questions.question: If speed doubles, travel time becomes:
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion halves the time.

questions.question: Ratios are useful in:
questions.explanation: Ratios are widely used in real-life situations.

questions.question: Which chart represents proportions visually?
questions.explanation: Pie charts represent proportions.

questions.question: If x and y are inversely proportional then:
questions.explanation: Inverse proportional quantities have constant product.

questions.question: If 3 pumps fill a tank in 6 hours, more pumps will:
questions.explanation: More pumps reduce the time required.

questions.question: Which ratio represents proportional ingredients?
questions.explanation: Both ratios simplify to 2:1.

questions.question: If the total angle of a pie chart is 360°, a category representing 1/4 will have:
Which is NOT an example of inverse proportion?
questions.explanation: Distance and time are directly proportional.

questions.question: If the ratio of red:blue:white paint is 2:3:5 and total paint is 20 litres, white paint is:
questions.explanation: White = (5/10)×20 = 10 litres.

questions.question: If x and y are directly proportional then:
questions.explanation: Direct proportional quantities have constant ratio.
